title: betula occidentalis subsp. fecunda
intro: Betula occidentalis subsp. fecunda, also known as the Western river birch, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its small stature and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Western river birch include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which make it useful in treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Western river birch can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
